commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add resource: Agents of Chaos

commit.message:
feat: 5 surgical LONG fixes — direction sanity gate, kill weak LONGs, raise floors

Data-driven fixes targeting LONG 32.2% WR while preserving SHORT 57.9% WR:

1. Direction sanity gate (new): blocks LONG if price falling (m1<0 AND m5<0),
   RSI>65 (overbought), or Binance leading down. SHORT only blocked in
   strong confirmed uptrend (all 3 timeframes positive). Fail-closed.

2. Kill weak LONG consensus: ALL weak_1of3 LONGs now blocked (57.5% of hard
   stops came from weak consensus LONGs at 0.189 avg confidence). Weak SHORT
   allowed only at confidence > 0.70.

3. Raise confidence floors: CONFIDENCE_FLOOR_LONG 0.55→0.65,
   SOLO_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR_LONG 0.65→0.75. Forces stronger agreement for LONGs.

4. Mean reversion: block LONG if momentum_15m < -0.0005 (don't buy falling
   knives). Preserves RANGING RSI 35/65 thresholds per GPT recommendation.

5. Prediction engine: relax SHORT RSI filter from <=55 to <=30 (only block
   SHORTs in deeply oversold territory). Unblocks good SHORTs that were
   being filtered too aggressively.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix bootfile client: parse response as text first, then try JSON

The Edge streaming response wasn't detected as text/plain due to
header differences. Now reads response as text unconditionally —
tries JSON parse (for cached bootfiles/errors), falls back to raw
text (for streaming responses).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct 3D mRoPE position_ids sharding in context parallelism (#1482)

mRoPE position_ids have shape [3, B, S] rather than standard [B, S].
Detect ndim==3 and shard along dim=2 instead of dim=1 to correctly
split the sequence dimension.

Signed-off-by: HuiyingLi <willwin.lee@gmail.com>

replaced non-existent `data-on:load` with `data-init` so the SSE stream
actually connects on page load.

  (mouse wheel), node drag, double-click fit-to-view, hover highlighting.
- Fix SPA navigation: bypass Datastar @get() (wasn't firing) and use

Make #content a flex column container so page layouts can use

Fixes conversations sidebar, context viewer sidebar, and IPC page
all scrolling independently from the main content area.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix padding by updating inline styles to include padding-left: 58px

Problem Fixed:
- CSS rules were being overridden by inline styles in the HTML template
- Inline style had `padding: 0 !important` which set ALL padding to 0
- This overrode the CSS `padding-left: 58px !important` rule

Solution:
- Updated inline styles to use explicit padding properties instead of shorthand
- Changed from `padding: 0 !important` to individual properties
- Now uses: padding-top, padding-bottom, padding-left, padding-right separately

Changes (sales_forecasting.html):

1. Line 449 - Template variation group td:
   OLD: style="padding: 0 !important; margin: 0 !important; ..."
   NEW: style="padding-top: 0 !important; padding-bottom: 0 !important;
               padding-left: 58px !important; padding-right: 0 !important;
               margin: 0 !important; ..."

2. Line 699 - JavaScript shop product breakdown td:
   OLD: style="padding: 0 !important; margin: 0; ..."
   NEW: style="padding-top: 0 !important; padding-bottom: 0 !important;
               padding-left: 58px !important; padding-right: 0 !important;
               margin: 0; ..."

Why This Works:
- Inline styles have higher specificity than CSS rules
- Shorthand `padding: 0` sets all four sides to 0
- By breaking it into separate properties, we can set left padding to 58px
- while keeping top, bottom, and right at 0
- The inline !important ensures it won't be overridden

Result:
- Inner variation tables now have 58px left indentation
- Aligns perfectly with Product Name column in parent row
- Top, bottom, and right padding remain at 0 as intended
- Clean visual hierarchy showing parent-child relationship

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: sign-out button uses full page reload instead of client navigation

Replace router.push('/login') with window.location.href = '/login' in
both landlord and tenant sign-out handlers. Client-side navigation via
router.push can fail silently when the Next.js router cache still holds
stale auth state, making it appear like nothing happened. A full page
reload ensures cookies are properly cleared and middleware re-evaluates.

Also adds type="button" to sidebar buttons and removes unused useRouter
imports.

Fix broken notification and CLI review URLs (#507)

## Summary
- Push notifications were sending users to `/review/{id}` and
`/instrumental/{id}` which 404 on Cloudflare Pages static hosting
- Remote CLI was opening browser to path-based URLs missing the `#/`
hash fragment
- Documentation referenced incorrect URL formats

All URLs now correctly use hash-based routing: `/app/jobs#/{id}/review`
and `/app/jobs#/{id}/instrumental`

## Changes
- **backend/services/push_notification_service.py** — Fixed notification
click URLs
- **backend/api/routes/jobs.py** — Fixed edit track response
`review_url`
- **karaoke_gen/utils/remote_cli.py** — Fixed CLI browser open URLs
- **karaoke_gen/utils/cli_args.py** — Fixed help text
- **Tests** — Updated assertions in push notification and remote CLI
tests
- **Docs** — Fixed URL examples in API.md, README.md, TESTING.md
- **pyproject.toml** — Bump version 0.137.0 → 0.137.1

## Test plan
- [x] Backend push notification tests pass (35 tests)
- [x] Remote CLI tests pass (174 tests)
- [x] Full backend test suite passes (103 tests)
- [x] Frontend unit tests pass (581 tests)

commit.message:
feat: add Semrush Keyword Gap scraper with Playwright and checkpoint recovery

- Playwright (Chromium non-headless) for manual login + XHR interception
- Intercepts Semrush API responses instead of DOM scraping (more robust)
- Checkpoint every 50 pages + resume on restart
- 3 retries per failed page with backoff delay
- Exports to XLSX (8 columns) + JSON backup
- Isolated package under scripts/semrush/ (own node_modules)
- run.ps1 launcher for PowerShell

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix matrix multiplication order in webgl-cube.html

mat4Mul(a, b) computes B*A (the inner loop iterates r[i*4+j] += a[i*4+k] * b[k*4+j],
which is column-major B*A). The previous call mat4Mul(proj, mat4Mul(view, model))
therefore computed model*view*proj instead of proj*view*model, placing
the cube behind the camera.

Fix: mat4Mul(mat4Mul(model, view), proj) correctly computes proj*(view*model).

Also add shader link error logging and a WebGL error check after draw.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Embed Info.plist in helper binary for Mach port rendezvous on macOS

Without a bundle identity, the subprocess looks up the Mach port
rendezvous server with an empty bundle-ID prefix, which doesn't match
the name the parent (vivid.app, com.vivid.app) registers. Embedding
Info.plist via -sectcreate makes CFBundleGetMainBundle() return the
correct bundle ID.

Also add GpuApp with --no-sandbox/--disable-gpu-sandbox (for future
multi-process use), and debug_record_type() helper for subprocess
diagnostics.
